data.executive_summary:
Net revenue reached $87,561, achieving 97.3% of goal with strong momentum driven by neuromodulator treatments generating $67,557 and representing 64.1% of service revenue. Marketing performance was particularly standout this month, with a lead-to-booking conversion rate of 42.5% — nearly triple the 15% target — and new patient ROI of 7.6x exceeding the 3x benchmark. The practice served 174 total appointments while maintaining a healthy membership base of 342 active members with an impressive 1.5% churn rate.

The clearest opportunity to support even more consistent growth lies in client retention, with the 180-day retention rate currently at 26%, below the 65% target. Strengthening follow-up protocols and encouraging longer-term treatment planning could help convert the strong new client acquisition into sustained relationships that drive more predictable revenue growth.

Performance varies significantly across the team, with Mandy operating at 99% utilization and generating $53,412 in net revenue with a $441 average order value, while other providers are operating between 47-64% capacity with lower revenue contribution. There's a meaningful opportunity to balance appointment distribution more effectively, potentially helping underutilized team members build their client base while ensuring the practice can accommodate demand without over-relying on a single provider.

assessments.title: Marketing ROI Performance
assessments.text: First-visit ROI of 7.6x significantly exceeds the 3x benchmark, generating $11,366 from $1,502 in ad spend. The 42.5% lead-to-booking rate is nearly triple the 15% target, demonstrating strong conversion efficiency.

assessments.title: Utilization Rate Strong
assessments.text: Utilization at 73.3% exceeds the 60% benchmark, indicating efficient capacity management. This strong utilization supports the high revenue performance at 97.3% of goal.

assessments.title: AOV Below Target
assessments.text: AOV at $354 achieves only 71% of goal, indicating potential revenue left on the table. Service bundling and upselling protocols could help capture additional value per visit.

assessments.title: Client Retention Deficit
assessments.text: 180-day retention at 26% is well below the 65% target, with 76% of appointments from new clients. Implementing systematic follow-up and treatment plan protocols is needed to improve client lifetime value.

assessments.title: Low Rebooking Rate
assessments.text: Rebooking rate at 33% falls below the 40% threshold, contributing to high new client dependency. Checkout rebooking training and incentive programs could improve appointment continuity.

data.psm_feedback:
**Your revenue performance at 97% of goal masks a fundamental retention problem that's costing you predictable growth.** With 180-day retention at just 26% against a target of 65%, you're essentially running a new client acquisition treadmill where 76% of your appointments came from new clients in March. While your marketing is delivering strong results with a 7.6x ROI and 42.5% lead-to-booking conversion, the low retention means you're constantly paying to replace clients who should be returning. Focus on implementing a structured follow-up sequence starting 30 days post-treatment to rebuild your returning client base.

**The AOV gap at 71% of target points to missed opportunities in treatment planning and service bundling.** Your average transaction of $354 leaves significant revenue on the table, particularly when Mandy demonstrates what's possible with a $441 AOV while maintaining 99% utilization. The concentration of 64% of service revenue in neuromodulators suggests clients aren't being educated about complementary treatments like dermal fillers or skin rejuvenation services. Train your team to present comprehensive treatment plans that address multiple aesthetic goals rather than single-service appointments.

**Staff utilization shows a clear performance divide that needs immediate attention.** While Mandy operates at full capacity with strong AOV, the remaining team averages just 53% utilization with AOVs ranging from $98 to $212. This disparity indicates either inadequate training in consultation skills or uneven client distribution. Implement peer shadowing sessions where lower-performing providers observe Mandy's consultation process, and establish minimum AOV targets with monthly tracking to elevate the entire team's performance.

**Your membership program shows strong fundamentals but isn't driving the retention it should.** With 342 active members generating $11,855 MRR and only 1.5% churn, the membership base is stable, yet overall client retention remains poor. This suggests members aren't using their benefits consistently or non-members aren't being converted effectively. Review your membership utilization data to identify inactive members for outreach, and train staff to present membership enrollment as the solution to treatment consistency during every consultation.
data.marketing_recommendations:
**Performance Summary**
March marketing delivered a **7.6x ROI** against the 3x goal, with $1,502 in ad spend generating $11,366 in attributed new patient revenue. The campaign produced **80 leads at $19 per lead**, resulting in 28 completed appointments from new patients. **Lead-to-booking conversion reached 42.5%**, well above the 15% benchmark, while the booking-to-completion rate held steady at 82.4%.

**Funnel Analysis**
While lead quality remains strong, the practice is losing approximately **6 booked appointments** at the completion stage each month. With a **$406 first-visit AOV**, these no-shows represent roughly **$2,400 in missed revenue** monthly. The 82.4% completion rate, though reasonable, presents the clearest opportunity for immediate revenue capture from existing marketing investment.

**Actionable Next Steps**
**Implement appointment confirmation protocols** 24-48 hours before scheduled visits, including text reminders with clear cancellation policies and rescheduling options. **Review new patient onboarding** to set proper expectations about appointment length, preparation requirements, and parking logistics. **Test offering flexible scheduling options** for new patients, such as extended evening or weekend availability, to reduce conflicts that lead to cancellations.

launches.title: Client tags
launches.description: Client tags give you and your practice high-level, at-a-glance context on every client right where you need it. You can add and remove tags directly from a client's profile, edit existing tag names and icons, create new custom tags with unique icons, and see client tags throughout your workflow from scheduling to invoicing.

launches.title: Payroll sync with time tracking
launches.description: Employee hours logged through Time Tracking can now sync directly into Payroll, eliminating the need to manually enter worked hours. This update removes one of the most time-consuming parts of payroll processing, resulting in a cleaner operational workflow and fewer errors.

launches.title: Online booking intervals
launches.description: You can now define the booking interval for your online booking calendar, which controls the appointment start times clients see when scheduling online. This gives you greater control over how appointments are scheduled, helping reduce awkward gaps in your calendar and align online bookings with your real-world workflow.

launches.title: Automatic terminal selection
launches.description: If your medspa has multiple payment terminals connected, Moxie Suite will now automatically select the last terminal used during your next in-person payment. This update helps reduce friction during busy checkout moments and keeps your team moving faster.

launches.title: Inventory brand management
launches.description: You can now view and edit product brands directly within your inventory for both service and retail products. Easily track products by brand, improve reporting visibility, and keep your inventory organized, especially helpful for practices managing multiple product lines.
